checks.a_analysis: The output accurately reflects the content of the source document. There are no fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or sections. All text found in the output can be traced back to the original PDF.
No phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs are present in either the source or the output document. No fabrication of contact information occurred.
The converted document includes all 14 pages and all sections from the source PDF, including both appendices and the references list.

I performed a row-by-row count for all tables in the document, including the multi-page 'Appendix A - Fields' table and the various 'Appendix B - Domains' tables. Every table in the output contains the correct number of rows as specified in the source document. For example, the 'NC Counties' grid on page 9 is fully represented as a 17-row table in the output, containing all 100 counties.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant text omissions were found. The prose in the introductory sections and the descriptions within the table columns are complete and accurately transcribed.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text capture is very precise. Technical terms, field names, and definitions match the source perfectly. Minor differences such as the handling of superscripts as trailing digits (e.g., 'Model2' instead of 'Model²') are considered acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text blocks. Each section and paragraph from the source document appears once in the JSON output, following the page-by-page structure.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent, particularly in Appendix B. In the source document, 'NC Counties', 'Place Type', and 'Placement' (on page 13) are sibling sub-sections within Appendix B. However, in the output, 'NC Counties' is not marked as a heading, 'Place Type' is assigned Level 2, and 'Placement' is assigned Level 3. This break in hierarchy prevents logical navigation via screen readers.
checks.b_evidence_source: The sub-headings 'NC Counties' (page 9), 'Place Type' (page 9), and 'Placement' (page 13) are all at the same level of hierarchy under 'Appendix B'.
checks.b_evidence_output: 'NC Counties' is a paragraph (element index RO 95), 'Place Type' is a heading level 2 (element index RO 97), and 'Placement' is a heading level 3 (element index RO 128).

checks.a_analysis: The table structure for 'Appendix A - Fields' is corrupted due to missing data. While the table is correctly started on page 4 and resumed on page 6, all rows corresponding to page 5 of the source (fields St_PosMod through Post_Comm) are entirely absent from the JSON output, replaced only by un-OCR'd image placeholders.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on page 5 containing fields: St_PosMod, LSt_PreDir, LSt_Name, LSt_Type, LSt_PosDir, Unit, St_Address, County, State, MSAGComm, and Post_Comm.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 5 (representing page 5) contains only two image elements (index RO 57, 58) and no table or data rows.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is significantly disrupted because an entire page of technical data (page 5) is omitted from the flow of the document. The transition from the end of page 4 (field St_PosDir) to the start of page 6 (field Post_Code) skips approximately 11 rows of critical attribute definitions.
checks.b_evidence_source: Technical content on page 5 defining legacy street name components and geographic attributes.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output jumps from 'St_PosDir' at the end of Section 3 (page 4) to 'Post_Code' at the beginning of Section 5 (page 6).

checks.a_analysis: The 'References' section on page 14 is a numbered list in the source document. In the output, this is converted to a single flat paragraph. While the text content includes the numbers and newlines, the structural meaning of a list is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered list 1 through 8 under the 'References' heading on page 14.
checks.b_evidence_output: A single paragraph containing the text: "1. AddressNC Steering Committee Member Listing\n2. NENA Standard..."

checks.a_analysis: No link injection issues were found. The document contains plain text references to external standards and lists, and the output reflects these as text. No fabricated links were added.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. The title page information, table of contents, and main body sections (Preface, Introduction, etc.) follow the same logical sequence. The use of a single-column layout for the body text and the addition of a responsive sidebar for navigation are appropriate for HTML and do not disrupt the document's flow or meaning. Information is located in the same relative positions as the source.

All tables, including the extensive 'Appendix A - Fields' table and the domain tables in Appendix B, are rendered correctly in the HTML. They maintain visible borders, aligned columns, and distinct header rows. The tables are clearly readable and the data relationship between rows and columns is preserved. The complex grid for 'NC Counties' is also successfully rendered as a grid.

The meaningful image from the source PDF (the map of North Carolina on page 1) is present and correctly rendered in the HTML output. There are no other meaningful images in the source document, and no broken image icons or missing elements are detected in the conversion.

There is no unintended duplication of body content. Each paragraph and table appears once in the body of the HTML document. The table headers in Appendix A repeat, which is a faithful reproduction of the source PDF's layout where these headers repeat on every page that the table occupies. The presence of headings in both the sidebar navigation and the main body is an intentional accessibility feature and not a defect.
